issue_title: az vm disk attach ignores caching argument
issue_body:
### Description
The argument "--caching" from "az vm disk attach" command is ignored. The disk is attached with host caching None

To reproduce the issue I ran the commands:
az group create  -l eastus -n topo-ene

az vm create -n topo-ene-vm-1 -g topo-ene \
--size "Standard_DS3_v2" \
--image RHEL \
--admin-username azureuser --ssh-key-value $ssh_key_path \
--storage-caching ReadOnly \
--storage-sku Premium_LRS \
--data-disk-sizes-gb 128

diskName=$(az vm show --name topo-ene-vm-1 -g topo-ene --query "storageProfile.dataDisks[0].name" )

az vm disk detach --name $diskName \
   -g topo-ene \
   --vm-name topo-ene-vm-1

az vm disk attach --disk $diskName \
   -g topo-ene \
   --vm-name topo-ene-vm-1 \
   --caching ReadOnly

I noticed that if I attach a new disk to the vm ( with --new argument), the caching is set correctly.

Shouldn't the caching argument be taken into consideration also for already created disks ?

Thanks a lot for your help!

issue_title: ssh-key-value and agent-vm-size get ignored while creating cluster using az acs create
issue_body:
I used the below script to create the cluster on Azure. The cluster gets created but it has the following problems:

1. **The SSH Key is not transmitted to the server even though specified.** (I tried both ways - file and value) So, when trying to login to the master with PuTTY, I get error. The workaround is to create the cluster with the below script, login to the Azure Portal and reset the password for the master by manually uploading the RSA Public key. Once, the password is reset by this method, it will allow access through Putty.

2. **The Agent VM size is ignored even though specified**. In the below script, I specified the Agent VM to be a Standard_DS1_v2. But the argument gets ignored and the Agent VM gets created with Standard_DS2_v2.
```
#!/bin/ksh

RESOURCE_GROUP=newexample
LOCATION=westus
DNS_PREFIX=newexample
CLUSTER_NAME=newexample

MASTER_COUNT=1
AGENT_COUNT=1
SSH_KEY_VALUE="$(cat $HOME/.ssh/id_rsa.pub)"

# Check this link for windows sizing https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/sizes-general
AGENT_VM_SIZE=Standard_DS1_v2

#Delete before start
az acs delete --resource-group=$RESOURCE_GROUP --name=$CLUSTER_NAME
az group delete --name=$RESOURCE_GROUP --yes

# Create group and cluster
az group create --name=$RESOURCE_GROUP --location=$LOCATION
az acs create --orchestrator-type=kubernetes --ssh-key-value "$SSH_KEY_VALUE" --resource-group $RESOURCE_GROUP --name=$CLUSTER_NAME --dns-prefix=$DNS_PREFIX --master-count $MASTER_COUNT --agent-count $AGENT_COUNT --agent-vm-size $AGENT_VM_SIZE

```

issue_title: After update to cli 2.0.42 command az group show --name changed behavior
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
The output of "az group show --name" when the group does not exist changed after updating from version 2.0.41 to 2.0.42.
This breaks all shell scripts depending on this output.


**To Reproduce**
With version 2.0.41 and earlier:
az group show --name test
Prints nothing, exit status is 0 when 'test' does not exist.
With version 2.0.42 prints 'Resource group 'test' could not be found.' and exit status is 3.

**Expected behavior**
I expect to have same behavior as before updating - no output and exit status 0.

**Environment summary**
CLI 2.0.42
Ubuntu 16.04 LTS

**Additional context**
Similar problem was noticed also for "az disk show" in earlier update.

issue_title: [Versioning] Mechanism to select command interface depending on API profile
issue_body:
Unresolved issue from PR #2526.

We should investigate having a way of defining and easily getting method signatures for different api versions. Something like:
```Python
storage_account_create_sig = {
  'profile1': (resource_group_name, account_name, location, account_type, tags=None),
  'profile2': (resource_group_name, account_name, location, sku, kind, encryption=None, access_tier=None, tags=None)
}
```
Then you have a way of getting an API specific signature for reflecting on parameters and so forth and you can call into a common implementation method by treating those signatures as kwargs.

One of the queries we're using bullet for is distance from point to `btCollisionObjects`.  There is a very real possibility that what we are doing is the wrong way around and would be considered an abuse of Bullet.  That said, we've discovered an error.  I'll provide a high level discussion of the error here and have code that reproduces the problem attached.

Test Scenario
==========
Create a shape (either sphere, box, or convex hull of the box).  It floats above the origin such that the distance from the origin to the nearest point on the shape is 0.25 units.  We then take a sequence of test points in the range [0.24, 0.25] (in this case, 101 samples so that the test point starts at a distance of 0.01 and moves closer by 0.0001 at each sample.)

At each query, we look at the known distance, a derived distance (the distance between the query point and the *nearest* point on the shape to that point), the reported normal from the shape to the query point.

When running the example on the command line, you can specify the test shape as a command line parameter, e.g.,
  - `./a.out sphere`
  - `./a.out box`
  - `./a.out convexhull`

The problem
==========

There are actually several problems:

1) For the box and convex hull, as the point gets closer and closer to the surface (in a monotonically decreasing manner), the reported normal direction flips directions arbitrarily.
2) It should be possible to compute the closest with high precision in these simple scenarios.  However, for the convex hull, we are getting an error in distance on the order of 1e-10-1e-12, and for the box, 1e-9-1e-10.  This last is particularly surprising.
3) When querying against the sphere shape, the point on the sphere given as closest is *always* the sphere center, regardless of the radius.

Example Code
===========
[bullet_point_query.zip](https://github.com/bulletphysics/bullet3/files/658483/bullet_point_query.zip)

Hi, first of all, thanks for the work on the project, it's very usefull to have an HMM framework in python. 

Regarding the disclaimer on the landing page: "For supervised learning learning of HMMs and similar models see seqlearn." 

What do you think about using the framework for a classification problem with labeled data?. The way I think it could work is by building one HMM per class (trained with the specific class data) and then, when data has to be predicted, compare the scores of each model and select the best. 

For example, in a two class classification problem, something like this: (didn't test it)

```python

from hmmlearn import hmm
import numpy as np

class CustomHMM:
  def __init__(self):
      def build_hmm():
          model = hmm.GMMHMM(n_components=3, n_mix=3, covariance_type="diag", init_params="t")
          model.transmat_ = np.array([[0.5, 0.5, 0.0],
                                      [0.0, 0.5, 0.5],
                                      [0.0, 0.0, 1.0]])
          return model

      self.hmm_0 = build_hmm()
      self.hmm_1 = build_hmm()

  def fit(self, X_train, y_train):
      # X_train shape(n_instances, n_samples)
      labels = set(y_train)
      if len(labels) != 2:
          raise Exception("y_train doesn't contain 2 classes")
      X_0 = X_train[y_train == 0, :]
      X_1 = X_train[y_train == 1, :]

      self.hmm_0.fit(X_0)
      self.hmm_1.fit(X_1)

  def predict(self, X_test):
      res = []
      for x in X_test:
           res.append(0 if self.hmm_0.score(x) > self.hmm_1.score(x) else 1)
      return np.array(res)
```
Then, you can use it:

```python
clf = CustomHMM()
clf.fit(X_train, y_train)
clf.predict(X_test)

issue_title: Bare and percent-encoded pluses in path variables become spaces in match_info
issue_body:
## Long story short

When match info is constructed for variable resource routes, ```'+'``` characters are converted to spaces. I do not believe this is the expected behavior.

Furthermore, even ```'%2B'``` codes are converted to spaces, limiting workarounds.

Note that similar behavior yarl's path parsing, reported in [yarl/issues/59](https://github.com/aio-libs/yarl/issues/59), has been corrected in [88799aec](https://github.com/aio-libs/yarl/commit/88799aec40231c6c06a28ad3c6f8a64fa835967e). A similar fix is needed in match_info parsing.

## Example

In our use case, the version string matched by the route is a semantic version that may include a plus sign followed by the build name, which violates the above assertion.

```Python
import aiohttp.web
app = aiohttp.web.Application()
async def handler(request):
    print(request.url)
    print('path: ' + request.url.path)
    print('match: ' + request.match_info['version'])
    # This assertion does not hold IF(F?) version contains a plus sign
    if request.url.path.endswith(request.match_info['version']):
        return aiohttp.web.HTTPAccepted()
    else:
        return aiohttp.web.HTTPExpectationFailed()
app.router.add_get('/resource/{version}', handler)
aiohttp.web.run_app(app, host='127.0.0.1', port=8088)
```
Suppose the handler is reached via a ```URL('http://server/resources/1.0.0+build')```:
```sh
curl http://127.0.0.1:8088/resource/1.0.0+build

# Note: this workaround fails too:
curl http://127.0.0.1:8088/resource/1.0.0%2Bbuild
```

### Expected behavior

Inside the handler, I expect the plus sign to be preserved in the portion of the URL path that has been captured by match info:
```Python
request.url.raw_path == '/resources/1.0.0+build'
request.url.path == '/resources/1.0.0+build'
request.match_info['version'] == '1.0.0+build'
```

### Actual behavior

Plus sign is preserved in the parsed path, but replaced by space in the match info:
```Python
request.url.raw_path == '/resources/1.0.0+build'
request.url.path == '/resources/1.0.0+build'
request.match_info['version'] == '1.0.0 build'  # UNEXPECTED
```

## Your environment

Xubuntu 16.04, python3.5, yarl==0.10.0, aiohttp==1.2.0 (but confirmed with aiohttp==2.0.7)

## Code inspection / suggested fix

The implementation of DynamicResource._match in aiohttp/web_urldispatcher.py is causing the problem.
 
https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/bcbceb5df5ddbe2fafae134be1b098d5ff8ab469/aiohttp/web_urldispatcher.py#L349

```Python
    def _match(self, path):
        match = self._pattern.fullmatch(path)
        if match is None:
            return None
        else:
            return {key: unquote(value) for key, value in
                    match.groupdict().items()}
```

Similarly to [88799aec](https://github.com/aio-libs/yarl/commit/88799aec40231c6c06a28ad3c6f8a64fa835967e) in yarl, if the ```unquote(value)``` is replaced with ```unquote(value, unsafe='+')```, then the issue I demonstrated above is fixed. I am unclear on whether '+:' should be used here as well. Nor am I confident that the same fix is needed in StaticResource (I believe it is) or other resources.

If someone more familiar with the code validates the approach and helps determine which resources need this, I am more than happy to compose and file a PR.

    await s.close()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(main())
```

## Your environment

aiohttp: 2.3.6 as client
OS: MacOS 10.13.1
proxy: squid

## Long story short

In very rare cases with heavily overloaded remote server aiohttp client leaks file descriptors for sockets on timeout.

## Expected behaviour

No leaked file descriptors on timeout.

## Actual behaviour

aiohttp client leaks FDs from timed out connections.

## Steps to reproduce

I don't have (yet) a way to reproduce the situation yet but I have some backtrace and code that generates it. It's from long-running HTTP client daemon which periodically fetches some resources.

```python
try:
    connector = aiohttp.connector.TCPConnector(
        resolver=Resolver(host, ip), verify_ssl=self.verify_ssl)
    with aiohttp.ClientSession(connector=connector) as client:
        r = yield from client.request(self.method, url, headers=headers)
        ...
except Exception as e:
    self.logger.info('failed to fetch', exc_info=e)
```

```
info 2017-02-22 23:30:20,927 : INFO : failed to fetch
Traceback (most recent call last):
File "/opt/fetcher/lib/python3.4/site-packages/fetcher/base.py", line 126, in _do_fetch
  r = yield from client.request(self.method, url, headers=headers)
File "/opt/fetcher/lib/python3.4/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py", line 577, in __iter__
  resp = yield from self._coro
File "/opt/fetcher/lib/python3.4/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py", line 274, in _request
  break
File "/opt/fetcher/lib/python3.4/site-packages/aiohttp/helpers.py", line 765, in __exit__
  raise asyncio.TimeoutError from None
concurrent.futures._base.TimeoutError
```

The issue happens very rarely when remote server is heavily overloaded and client sees all kinds of failures, including connections refused, resets and timeouts. There are no leaked FDs with other exceptions, only with this one.

Connections happen over HTTPS, remote server is Apache/2.4.25 (Amazon) OpenSSL/1.0.1k-fips PHP/5.6.29. Initially I noticed leaks with aiohttp 1.1.1 and thought it was caused by #1568 but upgrading to 1.3.3 didn't help.

Currently I'm running it with PYTHONASYNCIODEBUG=1 but so far no such timeouts yet. Any ideas how to debug that?

## Your environment

aiohttp 1.3.3, Debian Jessie, Python 3.4

issue_title: Provide the way to configure logging on C API level
issue_body:
Currently, libsovrin uses popular https://github.com/rust-lang-nursery/log facade. It allows linking with logger implementation in runtime.

We use env_logger that allows logging configuration by environment variables for our tests, but there is no logger initialization provided on C API level. I suggest the following:

1. Call env_logger init method during initialization of CommandExecutor singleton
2. Provide API that will allow registering custom callbacks for the logger. It will allow re-using same logging infrastructure as the main application that uses libsovrin.

Point 2 can be postponed.

issue_title: Brackets enclosing values (i. e. {{Institutional Author}}) are removed
issue_body:
The parser does a little bit too much cleanup. Entire values enclosed in brackets are stripped of the enclosing brackets. There are numerous instances where bracketing entire values are appropriate, for example with institutional authors. An example:

```
@book{SillyWalks1970,
 author = {{Ministry of Silly Walks}},
 location = {London},
 publisher = {We Publish Anything},
 title = {Handbook of Silly Walks},
 year = {1970}
}
```

The above bibtex entry yields (linebreaks added for readability):

```
[{u'publisher': u'We Publish Anything',
 u'title': u'Handbook of Silly Walks',
 u'author': u'Ministry of Silly Walks',
 'ID': 'SillyWalks1970',
 u'location': u'London',
 u'year': u'1970',
 'ENTRYTYPE': u'book'}]
```

Value of `author` should be `u'{Ministry of Silly Walks}'`, with brackets left untouched.

issue_title: currency filter fails if there's no active translation
issue_body:
```python
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'find'
```

This is because Django 1.7 used to return LANGUAGE_CODE if there's no active translation. Modify line 25 in `templatetags.currency_filters` to restore old behavior:
```python
        'locale': to_locale(get_language() or settings.LANGUAGE_CODE),
```

issue_title: rrule does not play well with pytz tzinfo at daylight savings?
issue_body:
Hi again, it's not clear if this is a dateutil bug, but it's at least a problem for users:

I parse a calendar with the `icalendar` package which returns datetimes localized with `pytz`.

Problem:
When using rrule to expand a recurring event, the occurences do not seem to respect Daylight savings. Here's a stand alone example:

```python
from datetime import datetime, timedelta,timezone
from dateutil.rrule import rrulestr
from pytz import timezone as pytz_tz
from dateutil import tz as du_tz
import dateutil.parser as dp

RRULE='FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=TU,TH;UNTIL=20180317T065959Z'
DTSTART='20180116T130000'

# localize datetime with pytz (like icalendar package)
zone = pytz_tz('America/Los_Angeles')
start = dp.parse(DTSTART)
start = zone.localize(start)

r=rrulestr(RRULE, dtstart=start)
n=datetime(year=2018, month=3, day=7,tzinfo=timezone.utc)
for d in r.between(n,n+timedelta(days=7)):
	print(d, d.tzinfo)
```

Output
```
2018-03-08 13:00:00-08:00 America/Los_Angeles
2018-03-13 13:00:00-08:00 America/Los_Angeles
```

But in America/Los_Angeles Daylight savings started on March 11, so I expect offset -07:00 on March 13.

If I convert the tzinfo of the start date to a dateutil timezone, by:

```python
# fix: replace tzinfo with corresponding dateutil tz
du_zone = du_tz.gettz(str(zone))
start=start.replace(tzinfo=du_zone)
```

The output is
```
2018-03-08 13:00:00-08:00 tzfile('/usr/share/zoneinfo/America/Los_Angeles')
2018-03-13 13:00:00-07:00 tzfile('/usr/share/zoneinfo/America/Los_Angeles')
```

But, for reference, with `zone = pytz_tz('America/Los_Angeles')` I get
```python
>>> str(zone.localize(datetime.datetime(2018,3,8,13)))
'2018-03-08 13:00:00-08:00'
>>> str(zone.localize(datetime.datetime(2018,3,13,13)))
'2018-03-13 13:00:00-07:00'
``` 
I cannot figure out at what point this information is lost.

Again, it is unclear to me if this is an error of pytz or dateutil, but either way it is likely of interest to the community. Thanks!

EDIT: I should say I do this in Python 3.6

issue_title: Add support for seconds from epoch prefixed by @
issue_body:
It would be nice if the parser would be able to read datetimes in seconds from the Unix Epoch.

One format to distinguish it from other numbers could be prefixing it with @ as supported by the `date` command as in e.g. 
`$ date --date='@2147483647'`
which, AFAIK, would be unambiguous.

I would find it useful because I'm writing a command line program that takes a date as an option, and in such context being able to express datetimes in seconds is usually expected.

issue_title: List field does does not put anything in valid_data if an item fails validation

issue_body:
I propose to deprecate the `Select` field in favor of a `OneOf` validator. Reasons:

* The `Select` field is just a validator masked as a field. It doesn't perform any real (de)serialization.
* It works only with basic data types. The field's `choices` argument can't be a list of "complex" objects (e.g. dates).
* Currently there is no built-in way to validate a field against a fixed set of values.
* A convenience method to associate choices to labels (e.g. to populate a form select field) would be quite useful. The validator could implement it in a generic way (i.e. not tied to any data type in particular).

Please let me know your opinion.

issue_title: Z3 answers a question with a question.
issue_body:
I stumbeled upon the following phaenomenon quite by accident:

I wanted to ask Z3 the following question:

question.smt2:
```
(set-option :produce-models true)

(declare-const b Bool)

(assert (= b (not (exists ((A Bool) (B Bool)) (= A B)))))

(check-sat)

(get-value (b))

(exit)
```

However, `z3 -smt2 question.smt2` yields the following output:
```
sat
((b (not (exists ((A Bool) (B Bool)) (= A B)))))
```

which is, in essence, not an answer, but my question. According to the SMTLIB specification, get-value is supposed to return a "value-term" for each given expression. For a boolean variable (b), I would have expected the result to be either "true" or "false", but not a formula, especially not one that contains quantifiers...

Could someone please help me figure out what is going on?

I am using Z3 version 4.6.0 - 64 bit on macOS Sierra Version 10.12.6 (16G1212).

issue_title: External References whose source_name includes 'capec' are invalid
issue_body:
The following should be valid STIX.  Notice that the source_name includes the string 'capec', but that should be legal

```
       {
            "source_name": "reference_from_capec",
            "description": "The Official Social Engineering Portal, Social-Engineer.org, Tick Tock Computers, LLC",
            "url": "http://www.social-engineer.org",
            "external_id": "REF-348"
        }
```

Here is the error message:

[X] attack-pattern--0a19a0a2-1b7b-4dde-99b9-624edc549928: external_references[0]: If the external reference is a CVE, 'source_name' must be 'cve' and 'external_id' must be in the CVE format (CVE-YYYY-NNNN+). If the external reference is a CAPEC, 'source_name' must be 'capec' and 'external_id' must be in the CAPEC format (CAPEC-N+). If the external reference is neither, it must contain the 'source_name' property and at least one of the 'external_id', 'url', or 'description' properties.

Changing the source_name to ```reference_from_CAPEC``` avoids this issue.

issue_title: Realsense ROS node stops publishing images
issue_body:
| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | D415, D435
| Firmware Version                   | 2.10.2
| Operating System & Version |  Linux (Ubuntu 16.04) | 
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)    |  4.13.0-38-generic, 4.13.0-36-generic | 
| Platform                                 | PC  |
| SDK Version                            |  2.10.2                          | 


## Issue Description
realsense ROS node stops publishing color images (didn't test with depth) on topic.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Start a realsense ROS node (as described in tutorial) publish color images on e.g. /camera1/image_raw
2. Start reading from the topic using e.g. `rostopic echo --noarr /camera1/image_raw`
3. Put your CPU under heavy pressure, by e.g. using ./cpuburn 

Now if you wait a couple of minutes, you will see at some point that there no new messages published on the topic.

Generally, it seems that the more realsense ROS nodes are on, the faster this phenomena occurs. 
The bigger CPU pressure, the faster this phenomena will occur. (I have 4 cores with hyperthreading, I usually put ./cpuburn -n 16)
It seems that this phenomena occurs faster if you keep subscribing to the topic:
`while [ 1 ]; do timeout 1 rostopic echo --noarr /camera1/image_raw`

I couldn't reproduce this on realsense-viewer.
The same phenomena occurs even if I set export OMP_NUM_THREADS=1
The same happens when instead of `rostopic echo` one uses python's rospy library.

### Workaround
1. Discover that no new images are published on the topic.
2. Kill the launch process (usually sending SIGINT is ok, at times you must SIGTERM or even SIGKILL)
3. Launch the process again.

In particular, this suggests that it's not a camera connectivity issue.

issue_title: CVE verification: Ensure last_override_config does not leak sensitive info
issue_body:
This Pulp CVE describes [issue 3521](https://pulp.plan.io/issues/3521) whereby the previous run's override config can be read as the attribute `last_override_config` on both importers and distributors. The decision (on the issue) was made to remove this attribute altogether due to it's security implications.

Without the fix, when you set an override_config, you'll be able to read that data when viewing the importer or distributor again. With this fix that data will always show up as `'last_override_config': {}`.

This should be verified for both Importers *and* Distributors.

Details on how I tested this during development (with commands) is here: https://pulp.plan.io/issues/3521#note-15

This is a backwards compatible change for 2.16.2 and a release note was added for it.

issue_title: Orphan remove by type can remove langpacks
issue_body:
To accomplish this test:

1. Sync a zoo repo with the feed: https://repos.fedorapeople.org/repos/pulp/pulp/fixtures/rpm/
2. Delete the zoo repo
3. List orphan
4. Orphan remove --type

I wonder what's the difference between GAN and GAIL regarding loss function.
In many documents, i saw it is computed like this:
```diff
-    generator_loss = tf.nn.sigmoid_cross_entropy_with_logits(logits=generator_logits, labels=tf.zeros_like(generator_logits))
+    generator_loss = tf.nn.sigmoid_cross_entropy_with_logits(logits=generator_logits, labels=tf.ones_like(generator_logits))
     generator_loss = tf.reduce_mean(generator_loss)

-    expert_loss = tf.nn.sigmoid_cross_entropy_with_logits(logits=expert_logits, labels=tf.ones_like(expert_logits))
+    d_loss_real=tf.nn.sigmoid_cross_entropy_with_logits(logits=expert_logits, labels=tf.ones_like(expert_logits))
+    d_loss_fake=tf.nn.sigmoid_cross_entropy_with_logits(logits=generator_logits, labels=tf.zeros_like(generator_logits))
+    expert_loss = d_loss_real + d_loss_fake
     expert_loss = tf.reduce_mean(expert_loss)
```
Example: [Alternative Loss Formulation ](https://wiseodd.github.io/techblog/2016/09/17/gan-tensorflow/)section.

As they say in this document, the Generator wants to maximize the probability of fake data (that's why it's set to ones and not to zeros) and the Discriminator, on the one hand, it wants to maximize the probability of real data (d_loss_real set to ones), and on the other, it wants to minimize the probability of fake data (d_loss_fake set to zeros).

Are the changes correct?
I don't understand why in baselines the generator tries to minimize the fake data.

Thank you in advance.

issue_title: Add logger for GraphQL execution exception
issue_body:
The current implementation catch all GraphQL execution exception and return as `errors` in response. However, the detail information of exception like line number, file name, call stack are missing. It would be difficult to debug when not having these information.

I think it could be better to have a new member `logger`, and when there is an execution exception, it can use `logger.exception` to log this exception for debugging.

issue_title: Isn't clear how to use the testing backend
issue_body:
So I've been using anymail for a while to send emails but now I've started work on tracking emails and I'm running into some issues around testing. The issue at the moment is that I've got lots of places in the code that read:

```
msg = self.generate_email()
msg.send()

email_sent = self.create_sent_object(email_id=msg.message_id)
```

Where generate_email is creating an EmailMultiAlternatives object. This is failing in tests because msg.message_id isn't working in tests. Presumably this is because Django is switching the email backend out on tests so they don't actually get sent. Based off what I've seen in this packages tests I've tried:

```
@override_settings(EMAIL_BACKEND='anymail.backends.test.TestBackend', ANYMAIL_TEST_SAMPLE_SETTING='sample',)
```

But that doesn't seem to be helping at all, also I have no idea what ANYMAIL_TEST_SAMPLE_SETTING is doing so 'sample' might be wrong. I can't seem to find anything in the docs covering what to do in this situation. Happy to write it up into the docs once I've got it working as it might be useful for other people.

issue_title: How to suppress automatic docstring generation
issue_body:
Is there a way to suppress the automatic generation of docstrings by pybind11?

I am converting my Python binding code from Boost.Python, which provides the [docstring_options](http://www.boost.org/doc/libs/1_61_0/libs/python/doc/html/reference/high_level_components/boost_python_docstring_options_h.html) facility for suppressing the creation of docstrings. I am wondering if something similar exists in the pybind11 framework.

Background: I want only class members to appear in my Sphinx generated documentation for which I explicitly provided a docstring. Members without a docstring should not appear in the documentation, because they are not part of the public API that I want to expose.

issue_title: invalid abstract return type when wrapping operator overloading on an abstract base class
issue_body:
## Issue description

It appears that `execute_cast` in pybind11's `operators.h` introduces an artificial requirement that the wrapped type be concrete and copyable (because `execute_cast` returns a plain instance).

```
pybind11/include/pybind11/operators.h:84:14: error: invalid abstract return type ‘Abstract’
     static B execute_cast(const L &l, const R &r) { return B(expr); }                  \
```

## Reproducible example code

```cxx
#include <pybind11/pybind11.h>
#include <pybind11/operators.h>

class Abstract
{
  bool operator==(const Abstract &other) const
  {
    return true;
  }
  virtual void doit() = 0;
};

class Concrete : public Abstract
{
  virtual void doit()
  {
  }
};

namespace py = pybind11;

PYBIND11_MODULE(mod, m)
{
  py::class_<Abstract>(m, "Abstract")
    .def(py::self == py::self)
    ;
}
```

Compiled with

```
 c++ -Ipybind11/include -I/usr/include/python3.6 t.cpp
```

gives

```
In file included from t.cpp:2:
pybind11/include/pybind11/operators.h: In instantiation of ‘struct pybind11::detail::op_impl<(pybind11::detail::op_id)25, (pybind11::detail::op_type)0, Abstract, Abstract, Abstract>’:
pybind11/include/pybind11/operators.h:59:24:   required from ‘void pybind11::detail::op_<id, ot, L, R>::execute(Class&, const Extra& ...) const [with Class = pybind11::class_<Abstract>; Extra = {}; pybind11::detail::op_id id = (pybind11::detail::op_id)25; pybind11::detail::op_type ot = (pybind11::detail::op_type)0; L = pybind11::detail::self_t; R = pybind11::detail::self_t]’                                                                                                      
pybind11/include/pybind11/pybind11.h:1103:9:   required from ‘pybind11::class_<type_, options>& pybind11::class_<type_, options>::def(const pybind11::detail::op_<id, ot, L, R>&, const Extra& ...) [with pybind11::detail::op_id id = (pybind11::detail::op_id)25; pybind11::detail::op_type ot = (pybind11::detail::op_type)0; L = pybind11::detail::self_t; R = pybind11::detail::self_t; Extra = {}; type_ = Abstract; options = {}]’                                                      
t.cpp:25:30:   required from here
pybind11/include/pybind11/operators.h:130:67: error: ‘bool Abstract::operator==(const Abstract&) const’ is private within this context
 PYBIND11_BINARY_OPERATOR(eq,        eq,           operator==,   l == r)
                                                                 ~~^~~~
pybind11/include/pybind11/operators.h:83:61: note: in definition of macro ‘PYBIND11_BINARY_OPERATOR’
     static auto execute(const L &l, const R &r) -> decltype(expr) { return (expr); }   \
                                                             ^~~~
t.cpp:6:8: note: declared private here
   bool operator==(const Abstract &other) const
        ^~~~~~~~
In file included from t.cpp:2:
pybind11/include/pybind11/operators.h:84:14: error: invalid abstract return type ‘Abstract’
     static B execute_cast(const L &l, const R &r) { return B(expr); }                  \
              ^~~~~~~~~~~~
pybind11/include/pybind11/operators.h:130:1: note: in expansion of macro ‘PYBIND11_BINARY_OPERATOR’
 PYBIND11_BINARY_OPERATOR(eq,        eq,           operator==,   l == r)
 ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
t.cpp:4:7: note:   because the following virtual functions are pure within ‘Abstract’:
 class Abstract
       ^~~~~~~~
t.cpp:10:16: note:      ‘virtual void Abstract::doit()’
   virtual void doit() = 0;
```

Just entering this for the record. I guess it is ok to close it if out of scope.

- docstring processing tools generally trim uniform amounts of indentation from the docstring, corresponding to the minimum indentation of all non-empty lines except the first one. See [PEP 257](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0257/#handling-docstring-indentation).
- C++11 supports multi-lines string literals with custom delimiters like
    ```
    R"mydelimiter(
        Cool Extension Module
        =====================
    )mydelimiter"
    ```
which are a really good fit for docstrings in pybind11.

- pybind11 inserts the signature at the first line of the docstring of functions and methods as it is customary for C extension modules, which cannot be inspected.

    `Signature : (x : array[float], y : array[float], z : array[float]) -> object`

Hence, if we don't indent the first line in the user provided docstrings, we end-up with a minimum common indentation of 0, which breaks the trimming.

Therefore, users willing to indent their C++ string litterals passed as docstrings should be careful to also indent the first line because of this insertion (and not do `R"(mydelimiter(Beginning of the docstring`).

*or*

pybind, which does *some* processing of the docstring (by inserting the signature) could trim it itself before inserting the signature.

issue_title: Linebreaks in chain.pem (and fullchain.pem) differ from previous versions
issue_body:
## My operating system is (include version):
Debian Stretch 9.4

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):
certbot-auto (0.22.0)

## I ran this command and it produced this output:
`./certbot-auto certonly --rsa-key-size 4096 --manual --preferred-challenges=dns-01 --manual-auth-hook /opt/eff.org/certbot/certbot_dns_authenticate.sh --manual-cleanup-hook /opt/eff.org/certbot/certbot_dns_cleanup.sh -d 'mydomain.tld','*.mydomain.tld' --server https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory`

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:
Yesterday I obtained a new wildcard certificate with the help of certbot. Everything went fine and it's working with NGINX and Dovecot like expected. But when I tried my script which is chaining it into a special form for my ZNC, I couldn't connect.
After debugging I noticed that the linebreaks differ from the certs I was obtaining in the past.
Namely chain.pem (and because of this also fullchain.pem) are different. The rest is like excepted.

previous chain.pem:
```
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----LF
xxxLF
yyyLF
-----END CERTIFICATE-----LF
```

new chain.pem:
```
LF
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----CRLF
xxxCRLF
yyyCRLF
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
```

Especially the missing linebreak at the end is breaking my application and I don't think it's like this on purpose. Resulting of this change the fullchain.pem is also different now.

issue_body:
To reproduce, start with an empty Certbot config directory and use any released version of Certbot to obtain an ACMEv1 prod cert. You shouldn't set `--server` anywhere.

Then checkout the `acmev2-default` branch and run `certbot renew --force-renewal`. You should see an error like:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/bmw/src/certbot/certbot/renewal.py", line 430, in handle_renewal_request
    main.renew_cert(lineage_config, plugins, renewal_candidate)
  File "/home/bmw/src/certbot/certbot/main.py", line 1190, in renew_cert
    le_client = _init_le_client(config, auth, installer)
  File "/home/bmw/src/certbot/certbot/main.py", line 641, in _init_le_client
    acc, acme = _determine_account(config)
  File "/home/bmw/src/certbot/certbot/main.py", line 508, in _determine_account
    acc = account_storage.load(config.account)
  File "/home/bmw/src/certbot/certbot/account.py", line 224, in load
    return self._load_for_server_path(account_id, self.config.server_path)
  File "/home/bmw/src/certbot/certbot/account.py", line 204, in _load_for_server_path

issue_title: Clarify the expected format of addresses in all functions in the codebase
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

In many places in the codebase we expect different formats of addresses. Either hex, or binary. We should clarify all these properly and try to keep it simple.

## Solution

I think we should establish the following:

- All API functions should accept a hexadecimal address, either with or without the `0x` prefix.
- This should be decoded by `address_decoder` into binary and used consistently around the codebase.
- Whenever we send addresses out into the world (e.g. as replies to the API) then again turn them in hex with `address_encoder`.

issue_body: Following https://github.com/pytrip/pytrip/issues/66#issuecomment-268022764

`sos execute task_id --kill`

The status file has the process id of the running process. For local subprocess-like jobs, we could try to kill the process and all its subprocesses. However, the jobs could be submitted to docker, or be executed (by PBS) on a remote worker, so there is no clear way how to kill them.

issue_title: Tensorflow performance degradation under heavy load due to hugepage compaction
issue_body:
<!--
General questions should be asked on the mailing list ray-dev@googlegroups.com.

Before submitting an issue, please fill out the following form.
-->

### System information
- **OS Platform and Distribution (e.g., Linux Ubuntu 16.04)**: Ubuntu 16.04
- **Ray installed from (source or binary)**: source
- **Ray version**: master
- **Python version**: 2.7
- **Exact command to reproduce**: ./train.py -f tuned_examples/pong-apex.yaml --redis-address=localhost:6379

<!--
You can obtain the Ray version with

python -c "import ray; print(ray.__version__)"
-->

### Describe the problem

Tensorflow performance degrades over time under high load. In the following graph, after a couple hours the time to compute gradients increases from ~60ms to ~800ms. This time is measured directly around the `sess.run` call into TensorFlow. If you try to get a timeline, the timeline does not show the latency.

![tf_slowdowns](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14922/36954463-07bfe4be-1fd7-11e8-96c0-dd5b0f5b146f.png)

I can reproduce this on a p3.2xlarge node with Ape-X running with 128 actors on m4.4xl nodes. If you run `sudo perf top` during one of these slowdown periods, you see a lot of time spent in hugepage compaction in the kernel:

```
29.85% [kernel] [k] pageblock_pfn_to_page
```

- If you disable hugepage compaction, performance improves but then after a bit the gradient latency increases to ~100ms. This is probably since the kernel is out of hugepages.
- If you disable transparent hugepages entirely, gradient latency increases permanently to ~100ms.

This issue is likely related to https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/issues/17315 which is triggering extra allocations when feeding data from read-only memory.

    import ray.serialization
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/ray-0.0.1-py3.6.egg/ray/serialization.py", line 6, in <module>

    from core.src.numbuf.libnumbuf import *
ImportError: /home/ubuntu/anaconda3/lib/libstdc++.so.6: version `CXXABI_1.3.8' not found (required by /home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/ray-0.0.1-py3.6.egg/core/src/numbuf/libnumbuf.so)
```

This seems related to #131 (which occurred with Python 3.5), but the error message has changed for Python 3.6.

Running `conda install libgcc` fixed the problem for me. Interestingly, after subsequently doing `conda uninstall libgcc`, the problem was still fixed.

    client.execute('DROP TABLE IF EXISTS test')
    client.execute('CREATE TABLE test (x Int32, y Int32) ENGINE=Memory')
    client.execute('INSERT INTO test FORMAT JSONEachRow', [record], settings={'input_format_skip_unknown_fields': skip})

issue_title: Allow setting source interface in open_tcp_stream
issue_body:
Right now `open_tcp_stream` has no option to control which outgoing interface the connections are made from. We should make this possible.

Subtleties:

* It needs to be possible to set this separately for AF_INET and AF_INET6
* We can't support setting the actual outgoing port, because we might need to make multiple outgoing connections in parallel

On Linux 4.2+, there's `sock.setsockopt(IPPROTO_IP, IP_BIND_ADDRESS_NO_PORT, 1); sock.bind((address, 0))` which means "delay picking the port until I call `connect`". We should use this if available. We should *not* use `SO_REUSEADDR`, because then it's possible to bind to a port that will fail when we actually call `connect` (because there might be a TIME_WAIT with the same 4-tuple).

So maybe something like: `source_host={AF_INET: "...", AF_INET6: "..."}`?

Should we also allow `source_host="1.2.3.4"` and auto-expand it into `{AF_INET: "1.2.3.4"}`?

If one is not specified, what happens? Maybe we should treat `INADDR_ANY` as meaning "you can use this protocol, and I don't care what host you originate from", and missing as meaning "don't use this protocol"? That could be nice since it would also give a way to say "I just want you to use IPv6", albeit not an obvious one.

Is it possible to create a new release/tag? The latest one if from January and there have been non-insignificant fixes since.

Thank you.

issue_title: Dev docs missing for AWS and Azure
issue_body:
Dev Docs are missing for both AWS and Azure. We need docs following the style in the slurm provider.
@benhg Could you help on this ?

Currently Parsl can automatically provision additional compute resources to match workflow needs. As for scaling down, we can only scale down to the min_blocks only when all blocks in a site are idle. Ideally, we want the scaling strategy to notice idle resources and release them so that we do not waste compute cycles. Here's our current documentation on [scaling](http://parsl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/userguide/execution.html#elasticity)


For an initial pass, the simplest useful case is where there's a single worker(ipp/swift-t) per block. In this case, a new [strategy](http://parsl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/devguide/dev_docs.html#strategy) could be added that supports polling the workers and identifies idle ones and terminates them.
There are some assumptions here:

1. This strategy only send shutdown messages to the workers, and does not explicitly cancel the blocks. We assume that once all workers in a block have exited the block itself will terminate.
2. We do not do any smart de-fragmentation.
3. We assume the cost of provisioning is low(or 0), and we don't do the cost comparison of freeing vs holding on to resources yet.

issue_title: OIDC auth refresh flow differs from kubectl behavior
issue_body:
I've been testing OIDC for authentication using the work done in this PR and came across an issue refreshing credentials because I do not specify `idp-certificate-authority-data` in my kubeconfig. This issue has already been brought up: https://github.com/kubernetes-client/python/issues/368#issuecomment-337778192. `kubectl` does not require this key. 

I don't think there should be an expectation to specify this key if you're using an IdP which uses a CA found in a trusted CA bundle. The request to the IdP's openid-configuration endpoint using the rest client falls back to the [Mozilla CA bundle if a certificate isn't specified](https://github.com/kubernetes-client/python/blob/369366a813964e3d72e91d402c5f471275e7a3e5/kubernetes/client/rest.py#L72).

Another potential issue is the lack of support for the `idp-certificate-authority` key which allows you to load CA certificates from a file. I was attempting to use this to get around the issue mentioned above by loading a trusted root CA bundle. I could convert the entire file to base64 and add it as a value for `idp-certificate-authority-data` but this results in an unwieldy kubeconfig file.

I'm using your library through the Ansible k8s_raw module and I'm getting the error `method read_namespaced_persistent_volume not found for model persistent_volume` when trying to create the following object
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolume
metadata:
  name: test
  namespace: test
spec:
  capacity:
    storage: 3Ti
  accessModes:
  - ReadWriteMany
  persistentVolumeReclaimPolicy: Recycle
  nfs:
    server: 1.2.3.4
    path: /Share
```
The module works correctly when the namespace item is omitted.

求个连续移动的接口，

Ideally, we should parallelize `prophyle_assignment.py` and find a way to balance the number of threads dedicated to `prophyle_index query` and to `prophyle_assignment.py`.

### 1st step
Profiling the two programs and, if `prophyle_assignment.py` is not the bottleneck, increase the default number of threads of `prophyle_index`.

### 2nd step
Parallelize `prophyle_assignment.py`.

### 3rd step
Come up with a way to balance the load of these two components of the classification pipeline. One way to do this is to define the number of threads for `prophyle_index` and use a thread pool of variable size in `prophyle_assignment.py` (it is probably easier to deal with load balancing in python than it is in C).
This would require a lot of testing though, and a good documentation.
Alternatively, we could use to separate options for thread # for `prophyle_index` and `prophyle_assignment.py`, but I am afraid this would make the program less user-friendly.

issue_title: sr_audit does not start for non-admin users.
issue_body:
sr_audit used to be only for administrative users.  But in later versions in 2018 ( > 2.18.03xxxx )
We have added that it looks for dead logs to figure out if user processes have died.  With the heartbeat processing, there should always be recent events in the log.

it doesn´t start up for non-administrative users...

```
peter@idefix:~$ sr_audit foreground
2018-05-29 11:40:52,757 [INFO] sr_audit has no admin set...
peter@idefix:~$ 
```

The Dueling Network implementation is using global mean and max, which calculates a 1,1 tensor over the whole training batch:
K.mean(a[:, 1:], keepdims=True)

I believe it should be:
K.mean(a[:, 1:], axis=1, keepdims=True)
